channel.title: 2026-06-22 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-22 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Advancing the Design of High‐Efficiency Printable Hole‐Conductor‐Free Mesoscopic Perovskite Solar Cells Through Machine Learning
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Science: Table of Contents
👤 Hao Meng, Jingzi Zhang, Xu Zhu, Yuelin Wang, Antai Yang, Kailong Hu, Chengquan Zhong, Jiakai Liu, Menghan Dun, Xi Lin
Advanced Science, EarlyView.

item.title: Spectral convergence of sum-of-Gaussians tensor neural networks for many-electron Schrödinger equation
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
An improved version of the sum-of-Gaussians tensor neural network (SOG-TNN) architecture is presented for solving the many-electron Schrödinger equation for one-dimensional soft-Coulomb systems. Model reduction techniques are introduced to reduce the number of tensor-factorized bases under the SOG approximation of the kernel. The Slater determinant ansatz is employed so that the antisymmetric property of the wave function can be strictly preserved. Numerical results show that the SOG-TNN achieves high accuracy with remarkably small basis sizes. Robust spectral convergence with respect to the basis size is also observed, consistently characterized by a mixed algebraic-exponential model for the error decay. These findings validate that the SOG-TNN architecture provides an ultra-efficient an…

item.title: Integrated Experimental-Machine Learning Framework for the Synthesis Optimization and Predictive Modeling of Iron Oxide Nanoparticles in Dye Removal Applications
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Omid Ahmadi, Hadis Behrouz, Reza Beigzadeh
This study presents an integrated experimental and machine learning framework for optimizing the co-precipitation synthesis of iron oxide nanoparticles (IONPs) and predicting their crystal size and specific surface area. A comparative evaluation of two precipitating agents showed that ammonium hydroxide produced superior results, yielding smaller crystal size (86 Å) and higher surface area (87.23 m²/g). A central composite experimental design was applied to model the effects of temperature (30-90 °C), stirring speed (100-600 rpm), and synthesis time (15-75 min) using Response Surface Methodology (RSM). The resulting models achieved high predictive performance, with R² values of 93.94% for crystal size and 92.40% for surface area. To improve model generalization, the dataset was expanded f…

item.title: Enhanced energy storage performance of confined co-doping dielectric films via nanoparticle self-assembly in ferroelectric phases
item.description:
📚 Nature Communications
👤 Kun Xing, Qi-Ze Han, Jian-Tao Wang, Lei Huang, Zhaoyu Ran, Li-Juan Yin 等13位作者
Nature Communications, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74471-5 The authors present a confined co-doping strategy for polymer-based dielectric films by incorporating BaTiO3 and Al2O3 nanoparticles into the ferroelectric core of coaxial electrospun fibers. The enhanced performance arises from the synergistic effects of high-polarization BaTiO3, wide-bandgap Al2O3, and BaTiO3/Al2O3 heterointerfaces.

item.title: Piezoelectric manipulation and stacking-dependent magnetism in ferrovalley materials
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Jia Li, Shiyao Zhang, Tong Wei, Xiong Yang, Limin Liu
The electronic transport characteristics of two-dimensional (2D) systems have widespread application prospects in the fabrication of multifunctional nanodevices. However, the current research for basic transport phenomena, such as anomalous valley Hall effect (AVHE) and piezoelectric response, is limited to discrete discussion. Here, we theoretically propose a valley-piezoelectricity coupling strategy beyond the existing paradigm to realize AVHE and layer Hall effect (LHE) in ferrovalley (FV) systems, and its essential principle can be extended to general valleytronic materials. Through first-principles calculations, we demonstrate that the large polarized electric field of 2.8×106 (1.67×107) V/m can be induced by 0.1% uniaxial strain in FV 2H-LaHF (1T-LaHF) monolayers. In addition, the m…

item.title: Decrease of magnetocaloric effect and magnetic susceptibility of ferromagnet due to magnetostructural phase transition
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Victor A. L'vov, Anna Kosogor
A theoretical model based on spin-exchange energy and the entropy of the magnetic subsystem provides a general framework for describing the temperature dependence of magnetization in systems undergoing magnetostructural phase transitions. By fitting the model to experimental magnetization curves, the key physical parameters governing these transitions are extracted. These parameters are used to calculate the magnetic-field-induced temperature change and magnetic susceptibility, including the paraprocess contribution. It is shown that, although magnetostructural transitions lead to a sharp change in magnetization, this alone is not sufficient to produce a giant magnetocaloric effect. Instead, the effect is strongly influenced by the magnetic susceptibility and the specific heat. The latter…

item.title: Sublayer-resolved altermagnetism in twisted antiferromagnetic bilayers with spin-layer coupling
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Kehan Liu, Zhihua Zhang, Yueheng Du, Haotian Sun, Mimi Dong, and Mingwen Zhao
Author(s): Kehan Liu, Zhihua Zhang, Yueheng Du, Haotian Sun, Mimi Dong, and Mingwen Zhao Twisting magnetic van der Waals materials provides a compelling approach to generate emergent spin textures through moiré engineering. In this study, we show that a twisted bilayer formed by two antiferromagnetic MnSe monolayers exhibits sublayer-resolved i-wave altermagnetic spin splitting. Combi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235429] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Hedgehog Weyl-like spin texture in the two-dimensional chiral SnP₂Se₆ semiconductor
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Lixin Zhou, Zhongjia Chen, Yuanjun Jin, Yingjie Wei, Yu Guo, Si Zhou, Yan Su, and Jijun Zhao
Author(s): Lixin Zhou, Zhongjia Chen, Yuanjun Jin, Yingjie Wei, Yu Guo, Si Zhou, Yan Su, and Jijun Zhao Two-dimensional (2D) semiconductors possessing strong spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and nontrivial topological features are central to the development of energy-efficient spintronics. While Weyl physics is extensively studied in semimetals, its realization in atomically thin semiconductors remains limit… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235431] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Mesoscale Domain Evolution Mechanism during Alternating Current (AC) Poling of Relaxor Ferroelectrics
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Yuan‐Jie Sun, Bo Wang, Long‐Qing Chen
Advanced Functional Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: Over‐Strain‐Relaxation State and Dislocation‐Governed Polar Topologies in Ferroelectric Superlattices
item.description:

item.title: Chemical tuning of magnetic ordering and cryogenic magnetocaloric response in zircon-type Gd₁−ₓErₓVO₄
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Ming Zeng, Muqing Su, Liang Ming, Xiaolong Yang, Wang Chen, Lingwei Li, and Hai-Feng Li
Author(s): Ming Zeng, Muqing Su, Liang Ming, Xiaolong Yang, Wang Chen, Lingwei Li, and Hai-Feng Li Chemical substitution offers an effective route to tune magnetic ordering and magnetocaloric performance in rare-earth (RE) oxides for cryogenic refrigeration. Here, we investigate the structural evolution, magnetic properties, and magnetocaloric effect of polycrystalline zircon-type Gd_… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214448] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Distinguishing polarization orientation via second-harmonic generation in the potential sliding ferroelectric SnP₂S₆
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Fengfeng Ye, Qiankun Li, Zhuocheng Lu, Xinfeng Chen, Yang Li, Hua Wang, Lu You, and Gaoyang Gou
Author(s): Fengfeng Ye, Qiankun Li, Zhuocheng Lu, Xinfeng Chen, Yang Li, Hua Wang, Lu You, and Gaoyang Gou As emerging ferroelectric (FE) materials, ultrathin two-dimensional (2D) sliding FEs without a phase-matching bottleneck usually exhibit pronounced second-harmonic generation (SHG) responses. Despite that the structural polarity of sliding FEs can be precisely detected via SHG characterizations, dis… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214111] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Effects of electric field rising time on polarization switching in ferroelectrics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Bowen Li, Jinhong Li, Wenpeng Zhu, Haohua Wen, and Yue Zheng
Author(s): Bowen Li, Jinhong Li, Wenpeng Zhu, Haohua Wen, and Yue Zheng The electric field rising time tᵣ is frequently overlooked in ultrafast ferroelectric switching studies, despite its critical role in switching dynamics and mechanism discrimination. Using stochastic phase-field simulations of BaTiO₃, we systematically investigate tᵣ effe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224112] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Multiscale study of various topological spin textures in two-dimensional materials
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Nan Fei, Yan Luo, Jiabao Liao, Jia Luo, Lei Gu, Jing Xia, Xue Liang, and Guoping Zhao
Author(s): Nan Fei, Yan Luo, Jiabao Liao, Jia Luo, Lei Gu, Jing Xia, Xue Liang, and Guoping Zhao The development of two-dimensional (2D) magnetic materials provides an ideal platform for the discovery and manipulation of topological spin textures. However, most 2D materials can only support one kind of spin textures, which severely limits their diversity and potential applications. In this stud… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214446] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Marginal influence of anomalous Josephson current on odd-frequency spin-triplet pairing in ferromagnetic Josephson diodes
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Subhajit Pal and Colin Benjamin
Author(s): Subhajit Pal and Colin Benjamin We examine how an anomalous Josephson current influences odd-frequency superconducting correlations in two Josephson junction geometries. The first consists of two ferromagnetic layers between conventional s-wave superconductors, with magnetizations along the x and y axes, forming a bilayer ju… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214516] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Enabling Record‐Low Coercive Field and Large Polarization in Hybrid Germanium Iodide Ferroelectric Through Chemical Bonding Engineering

item.title: Record-high Glass coefficient in the shift current response of a ferroelectric halide perovskite
item.description:
📚 PNAS
👤 Koma MikiMasao NakamuraAsahi YamadaGurvan BosserKiyohiro AdachiDaisuke HashizumeNaoki OgawaSatoshi OkamotoYoshinori TokuraMasashi Kawasakiahttps://ror.org/057zh3y96Department of Applied Physics and Quantum-Phase Electronics Center, The University of Tokyo, Tokyo 113-8656, Japanbhttps://ror.org/057zh3y96Department of Fundamental Engineering, Institute of Industrial Science, The University of Tokyo, Tokyo 153-8505, Japanchttps://ror.org/03gv2xk61RIKEN Center for Emergent Matter Science, Wako 351-0198, JapandRIKEN Baton Zone Program, Wako 351-0198, Japanehttps://ror.org/01dq60k83Department of Physics, Graduate School of Science, Tohoku University, Sendai 980-8578, JapanfSumitomo Chemical Co., Ltd, Tokyo 103-6020, Japanghttps://ror.org/057zh3y96Tokyo College, The University of Tokyo, Tokyo 113-8656, Japan
Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, Volume 123, Issue 26, June 2026. SignificanceShift current is a second-order nonlinear optical effect arising from the change in the quantum geometry of wave functions, and provides a photovoltaic mechanism fundamentally distinct from conventional junction-based photovoltaics. CsGeI3is ...

item.title: Emergent partons in fractional quantum Hall systems
item.description:
📚 Nature Physics
👤 Zihao Yang, Yifan Wang, Xinyu Lu, Ziyu Liu, Kirk W. Baldwin, Ken W. West 等10位作者
Nature Physics, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03338-9 In a geometrical theory for the fractional quantum Hall effect, chiral spin-2 neutral excitations arise. Now, multiple such excitations are observed and provide evidence for the parton description of the quantum Hall effect.

item.title: Forty years of high-temperature superconductivity
item.description:
📚 Nature
👤 Inna Vishik, Warren Pickett
Nature, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01801-4 The first demonstration of superconductivity at 35 kelvin drove decades of materials research and introduced a puzzle about this strange state of matter.

item.title: PI-SONet: A Physics-Informed Symplectic Operator Network for Real-Time Optimal Control of Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Jerome Darbon, Alan John Varghese, Shanqing Liu, Paula Chen, Yaochen Zhu, George Karnadiakis
Many real-life applications involve controlling high-dimensional multi-agent systems in real-time. Existing optimal control solvers often suffer from the curse-of-dimensionality and require complete rerunning for each new problem setting. We target nonconvex, nonlinear problems in 100s of dimensions by introducing PI-SONet (Physics-Informed Symplectic Operator Network), a structure-preserving operator learning framework for solving parameterized families of optimal control problems and their Pontraygin Maximum Principle (PMP) systems. PI-SONet combines a latent right-space solver with a conditional symplectic operator to produce tractable Hamiltonian trajectories in a computationally efficient auxiliary space and transform them back to physical space. This decomposition yields a single tr…

item.title: Multiband superconductivity in the kagome-lattice superconductor Re₂Zr
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Gabriel Kuderowicz and Bartlomiej Wiendlocha
Author(s): Gabriel Kuderowicz and Bartlomiej Wiendlocha We present a first-principles investigation of the electronic structure, lattice dynamics, electron-phonon coupling, and superconducting properties of the hexagonal kagome-lattice compound Re₂Zr, in which time-reversal symmetry breaking and unconventional pairing were recent… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224521] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Magnetic fluctuations and anisotropy in UTe₂: A multi-orbital study based on GGA+U and RPA
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Makoto Shimizu and Youichi Yanase
Author(s): Makoto Shimizu and Youichi Yanase Pressure-induced changes in the magnetic and superconducting properties of a spin-triplet superconductor candidate UTe₂ have attracted considerable interest, underscoring the need for microscopic theoretical insight. In this paper, we investigate magnetic fluctuations and their aniso… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235137] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Nonlinear phononics in LaFeAsO: Optical control of the crystal structure toward possible enhancement of superconductivity
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Shu Kamiyama, Tatsuya Kaneko, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Masayuki Ochi
Author(s): Shu Kamiyama, Tatsuya Kaneko, Kazuhiko Kuroki, and Masayuki Ochi Nonlinear phononics provides a route to control crystal structures through light-induced phonon excitation. In this study, we apply nonlinear phononics to an iron-based superconductor, LaFeAsO, with the aim of tuning its crystal structure toward the ideal one to enhance superconductivity. We simulat… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224315] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Gradient scalability and Taylor surrogation of quantum cost landscapes
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Sabri Meyer, Francesco Scala, Francesco Tacchino, and Aurelien Lucchi
Author(s): Sabri Meyer, Francesco Scala, Francesco Tacchino, and Aurelien Lucchi Variational quantum algorithms (VQAs) are promising candidates for near-term quantum computing, yet they face scalability challenges due to barren plateaus, where gradients vanish exponentially in the system size. Recent conjectures suggest that avoiding barren plateaus might inherently lead to clas… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023325] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Interplay among spin, orbital, and lattice degrees of freedom in the magnetocaloric compound HoB₂
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Noriki Terada, Simon R. Larsen, Takafumi D. Yamamoto, Daisuke Okuyama, Hironori Nakao, Ginga Kitahara, Shuki Torii, Hiraku Saito, Taro Nakajima, Osamu Sakai, Hiroaki Mamiya, Kensei Terashima, Hiroyuki Takeya, Yoshihiko Takano, and Hideaki Kitazawa
Author(s): Noriki Terada, Simon R. Larsen, Takafumi D. Yamamoto, Daisuke Okuyama, Hironori Nakao, Ginga Kitahara, Shuki Torii, Hiraku Saito, Taro Nakajima, Osamu Sakai, Hiroaki Mamiya, Kensei Terashima, Hiroyuki Takeya, Yoshihiko Takano, and Hideaki Kitazawa We investigated the magnetic ordering, crystal lattice distortion, and spin dynamics in the magnetocaloric material HoB₂ using neutron and x-ray diffraction (XRD), along with AC susceptibility measurements. Although HoB₂ exhibits a ferromagnetic phas… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224436] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Dielectric screening in electromagnetic dressing of semiconductors
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Quentin Courtade, Umberto Dellasette, Sotirios Fragkos, Stéphane Petit, Dominique Descamps, Yann Mairesse, and Samuel Beaulieu
Author(s): Quentin Courtade, Umberto Dellasette, Sotirios Fragkos, Stéphane Petit, Dominique Descamps, Yann Mairesse, and Samuel Beaulieu Nonequilibrium manipulation of quantum materials via electromagnetic dressing provides an on-demand route to tailoring electronic band structures through Floquet engineering. Time- and angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy offers a direct means to probe these light-dressed electronic states. In … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235430] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Piezochromism, amorphization, and polyamorphic superconductivity in one-dimensional van der Waals InSeI under high pressure
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Ruijing Liang, Min Zhang, Chao An, Ying Zhou, Yonghui Zhou, Shuyang Wang, Zengming Zhang, and Zhaorong Yang
Author(s): Ruijing Liang, Min Zhang, Chao An, Ying Zhou, Yonghui Zhou, Shuyang Wang, Zengming Zhang, and Zhaorong Yang Chalcohalides compounds represent a promising class of optoelectronic materials. Among them, QSeI (Q=In, Ga, Al) stands out due to its one-dimensional van der Waals structure and helical chain configuration. In this study, we investigate the pressure-tunable o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224114] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Zeeman-activated Berry-curvature magnetotransport from the bulk of nonmagnetic metals with inversion symmetry
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Rhonald Burgos Atencia, Antonio Vecchione, Denys Makarov, and Carmine Ortix
Author(s): Rhonald Burgos Atencia, Antonio Vecchione, Denys Makarov, and Carmine Ortix The Berry curvature (BC)—a quantity encoding the geometry of electronic wavefunctions—governs various electronic transport effects in quantum materials. In magnetic systems, the BC is reponsible for the intrinsic part of the anomalous Hall conductivity. Local concentrations of BC in noncentrosymmetr… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235134] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Ligand-mediated magnetic coupling across metamagnetic transitions in CrPS₄
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Giuseppe Buccoliero, Rachel Nickel, Roberto Sant, Marli dos Reis Cantarino, Andrei Rogalev, Nathan J. Yutronkie, Tristan Riccardi, Daniel A. Chaney, Kurt Kummer, Johann Coraux, and Nicholas B. Brookes
Author(s): Giuseppe Buccoliero, Rachel Nickel, Roberto Sant, Marli dos Reis Cantarino, Andrei Rogalev, Nathan J. Yutronkie, Tristan Riccardi, Daniel A. Chaney, Kurt Kummer, Johann Coraux, and Nicholas B. Brookes Chromium thiophosphate (CrPS₄) is a long-known material: A layered semiconducting antiferromagnet. Its recently discovered gate-tunable metamagnetic phase transitions, the remarkable positive and oscillating magnetoresistance as a tunnel barrier, and its Fano-resonance luminescence, … [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023323] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Sensitivity enhancement in superconducting-qubit magnetometry via two-level systems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Azat Gubaydullin, Vladimir Slepnev, Valerii Vinokur, and Florian Neukart
Author(s): Azat Gubaydullin, Vladimir Slepnev, Valerii Vinokur, and Florian Neukart We study quantum magnetometry based on phase-estimation algorithms in a sensor formed by a superconducting qubit coupled to a two-level system (TLS). Using Ramsey interferometry with single-shot binary readout, we construct a precomputed calibration pattern P₀(fq,τ,ϕₘ) and implement… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224522] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Electronic orbital response to static magnetic fields. II. A general theoretical method
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
As a part of an ongoing project devoted to the development of theoretical foundations and computational methods for treating systems in external electro-magnetic fields, we present a new method here for dealing with magnetic fields of arbitrary strength and for arbitrary systems. The method is based on leaving the commonly used Coulomb gauge and instead introducing an operator gauge. We show that this method avoids the obstacles related with using the Coulomb gauge in combination with GIAOs (gauge-invariant atomic orbitals) as basis functions, i.e., complicated matrix elements and oscillatory behavior for non-magnetic terms. Moreover, our approach shares many features with the “Modern Theory of Magnetization,” which is based on the operator, ∇⃗k. However, our approach is not restricted to…

item.title: Quantum theory for edge current and noise in two-dimensional topological superconductors
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 S. Pintus and A. Crépieux
Author(s): S. Pintus and A. Crépieux We calculate the edge current and its fluctuations, i.e., noise, in a two-dimensional topological superconductor using the T-matrix and the Green's function techniques. We show that the current is zero for nonchiral edge states and nonzero for chiral edge states, while the edge noise is nonzero wh… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235428] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum dynamical signatures of topological flow transitions in limit cycle phases
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Alejandro S. Gómez and Javier del Pino
Author(s): Alejandro S. Gómez and Javier del Pino Quantum self-oscillatory phases are ubiquitous in driven-dissipative systems. Classically, each phase is defined by its flow pattern and how stationary sets organize phase space (e.g., fixed points and limit cycles), with transitions triggered by local bifurcations or global basin rearrangements. In… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023319] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Topological and nontopological defects from quantum reset dynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 R. Jafari, Henrik Johannesson, and Sebastian Eggert
Author(s): R. Jafari, Henrik Johannesson, and Sebastian Eggert We analyze mechanisms for universal out-of-equilibrium dynamics near criticality by exploring the effect of randomized quantum resetting (QR) under a linear finite-time drive across, or very close to, an equilibrium quantum critical point. Using the transverse-field Ising chain as a minimal model an… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224312] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Enhanced plasma production by ambipolar electric fields in convergent magnetic fields
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Kazunori Takahashi, Christine Charles, Rod W. Boswell, and Antonella Caldarelli
Author(s): Kazunori Takahashi, Christine Charles, Rod W. Boswell, and Antonella Caldarelli Controlled unidirectional remote plasma production in a radio-frequency (rf) plasma source is demonstrated by placing magnetic nozzle throat several tens of centimeters away from the rf antenna, where a local density peak forms near the throat. The experiment indicates that the dominant electron ene… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023322] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Finite-bath open quantum systems: Exact dynamics
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
In this study, we introduce a method for deriving exact master equations from the dynamical map for finite open quantum systems coupled to (in)finite reservoirs, using the principle of minimal dissipation. The exact dynamics of the central spin model, which models a finite-bath open quantum system, is developed for two interaction types: Heisenberg and stochastic pure-dephasing interactions. The Heisenberg interaction yields a novel phase-covariant quantum channel in the strong-coupling regime, offering a new platform for studying a range of quantum information protocols. The stochastic pure-dephasing interaction provides the microscopic derivation of the paradigmatic non-Markovian random telegraph noise (RTN) channel, establishing its quantum foundation and offering insight into stochast…

item.title: Negative temperature coefficient of Gilbert damping in magnetic bilayers
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 Lulu Cao, Yuting Gong, Xianyang Lu, Yongbing Xu, Ya Zhai, Jing Wu, Roy W. Chantrell, and Richard F. L. Evans
Author(s): Lulu Cao, Yuting Gong, Xianyang Lu, Yongbing Xu, Ya Zhai, Jing Wu, Roy W. Chantrell, and Richard F. L. Evans In simple metallic magnets, the intrinsic Gilbert damping increases with temperature and diverges near the Curie temperature as a result of spin fluctuations. Here, the authors find surprising and opposite behavior in Py/Nd bilayers, where the Gilbert damping decreases with increasing temperature and can be controlled by varying the thickness of the Nd capping layer. Their findings present a new spintronic spin-pumping effect that can be used to modify the dynamic properties of nanoscale devices for improved switching dynamics. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L220409] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: High-speed magnetic-field imaging via N-V ensemble and laser raster scanning
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Applied
👤 Luca Troise, Nikolaj W. Hansen, Marvin Holten, Dhiren Kara, Annika Pörner, Jean-François Perrier, Ulrik L. Andersen, and Alexander Huck
Author(s): Luca Troise, Nikolaj W. Hansen, Marvin Holten, Dhiren Kara, Annika Pörner, Jean-François Perrier, Ulrik L. Andersen, and Alexander Huck We present a technique based on an ensemble of nitrogen-vacancy (N-V) centers in diamond capable of imaging magnetic fields with high spatiotemporal resolution and sensitivity. A focused laser beam is raster scanned using an acousto-optic deflector and N-V center fluorescence is read out with a … [Phys. Rev. Applied 25, 064065] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Optimal filtering and generation of entangled photons for quantum applications in the presence of noise
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Applied
👤 Jordan M. Thomas, Andrew R. Cameron, Akil Pathiranage, Si Xie, Raju Valivarthi, Panagiotis Spentzouris, Maria Spiropulu, Cristián Peña, and Prem Kumar
Author(s): Jordan M. Thomas, Andrew R. Cameron, Akil Pathiranage, Si Xie, Raju Valivarthi, Panagiotis Spentzouris, Maria Spiropulu, Cristián Peña, and Prem Kumar The distribution of quantum entanglement and teleportation in real-world environments underlies current efforts in quantum communication and networking, and requires designing devices such that extraneous noise photons do not obscure photon detection. This study analyzes the physics of filtering entangled-photon sources for both high noise rejection and purity, for multiphoton applications in high-noise scenarios. Using these methods, entanglement is successfully distributed through 50 km of optical fiber while coexisting high-power classical Internet signals generate substantial background noise. [Phys. Rev. Applied 25, 064064]…

item.title: Structural and magnetic properties of epitaxially strained (001)- and (111)-oriented Pb₂CoTeO₆ from first-principles
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Md Kamal Hossain and Elizabeth A. Nowadnick
Author(s): Md Kamal Hossain and Elizabeth A. Nowadnick The application of epitaxial strain to perovskite thin films has emerged as a powerful tool for tuning their properties, with differences observed between (001)- and (111)-oriented films. Although these effects have been extensively studied in ABO₃ perovskites, the influence of strai… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224109] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Anisotropic vortex motion and two-dimensional superconducting transition
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Zhipeng Xu, Kun Jiang, and Jiangping Hu
Author(s): Zhipeng Xu, Kun Jiang, and Jiangping Hu Vortex motion plays a central role in determining the resistance of two-dimensional superconductors, both in the context of the Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless (BKT) transition and in the mixed state of type-II superconductors under magnetic fields. In this study, we introduce an anisotropic pinning… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214517] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Entropy landscape and field-tuned thermodynamics of the large-spin Shastry-Sutherland magnet Eu₂MgSi₂O₇
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Huanpeng Bu, Han Ge, Fangli Li, Lei Xu, Jiayue Yuan, Tiantian Li, Junzhe Liu, Jian Chen, Ying Fu, Nan Zhao, Zhongwen Ouyang, Ping Miao, Jieming Sheng, and Liusuo Wu
Author(s): Huanpeng Bu, Han Ge, Fangli Li, Lei Xu, Jiayue Yuan, Tiantian Li, Junzhe Liu, Jian Chen, Ying Fu, Nan Zhao, Zhongwen Ouyang, Ping Miao, Jieming Sheng, and Liusuo Wu We present a comprehensive study of the low-temperature magnetism and field-tuned thermodynamics of the layered Shastry-Sutherland compound Eu₂MgSi₂O₇. Eu²⁺ ions (4f⁷, S=7/2) form a quasi-two-dimensional orthogonal-dimer network, rea… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214447] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Identification and optimization of accurate spin models for Fermi-Hubbard ladders using matrix product states
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Andoni Agirre, Thomas Frederiksen, Géza Giedke, and Tobias Graß
Author(s): Andoni Agirre, Thomas Frederiksen, Géza Giedke, and Tobias Graß Open-shell nanographenes offer a controlled setting to study correlated magnetism emerging from π-electron systems. Here, we study non-bipartite Fermi-Hubbard ladders describing oligo(indenoindene) molecules. These feature a gapped, weakly dispersing manifold of quasizero modes in their single-par… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 245422] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Zeeman-like coupling to valley degree of freedom in Si-based spin qubits
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 S. A. Jafari, Hendrik Bluhm, and David P. DiVincenzo
Author(s): S. A. Jafari, Hendrik Bluhm, and David P. DiVincenzo Increasing the valley splitting in Si-based heterostructures is critical for improving the performance of semiconductor qubits. This paper establishes that the two low-energy conduction band valleys are not independent parabolic bands. Instead, they originate from the X point of the Brillouin zone, … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 245204] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Tensor network loop cluster expansions for quantum many-body problems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Johnnie Gray, Gunhee Park, Glen Evenbly, Nicola Pancotti, Eirik F. Kjønstad, and Garnet Kin-Lic Chan
Author(s): Johnnie Gray, Gunhee Park, Glen Evenbly, Nicola Pancotti, Eirik F. Kjønstad, and Garnet Kin-Lic Chan We analyze the tensor network loop cluster expansion, introduced by G. Park et al. [ Phys. Rev. B 112 , 174310 (2025) ] as a systematic correction to belief propagation, in the context of general quantum many-body problems. We provide numerical examples of the accuracy and practical applicability of t… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235135] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Atomistic approach to exciton-phonon couplings in semiconductor quantum dots
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Yasser Saleem and Moritz Cygorek
Author(s): Yasser Saleem and Moritz Cygorek We present a fully atomistic approach to exciton-phonon coupling in semiconductor quantum dots that bridges microscopic electronic-structure calculations with non-Markovian open-quantum-system dynamics. On the example of an InAsP quantum dot embedded in an InP matrix, we compute single-particle stat… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235139] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Deterministic, dynamically reconfigurable single quantum emitters enabled by tip-enhanced nano-optical trapping spectroscopy
item.description:
📚 Nature Communications
👤 Yeonjeong Koo, Jaehun Shin, Jonggeun Hwang, Hyeongwoo Lee, Hyeonmin Oh, Sujeong Kim 等13位作者
Nature Communications, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74532-9 A tip-enhanced nano-optical trapping spectroscopy enables deterministic trapping and dynamic control of single quantum emitters, including reversible switching between weak and strong light-matter coupling regimes.

item.title: Photophysical Insights of Blue–Green Emitting Oxadiazole: Efficient Energy Transfer in Core–Shell Quantum Dot Hybrids
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Sushma Katti, Amith Yadav H J, G H Pujar, S R Inamdar, Narahari Deshapande, Vinay Parol 等9位作者
The development of blue–green emitting organic chromophores with excellent optoelectronic properties remains an important issue. In this context, the synthesis of a novel blue–green emitting chromophore, 2-(4-(2-(anthracen-10-yl)vinyl)phenyl)-5-(4-tert-butylphenyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole (AVBO), was accomplished via the donor–π–acceptor (D- π–A) strategy using a Wittig reaction. The integration of aryl-heteroaryl groups and the oxadiazole group was utilized to obtain efficient blue–green emission with excellent optical properties. AVBO exhibited stable and efficient PL in the range of 485–510 nm across diverse solvent environments. Solvatochromic analysis using multiple correlations (Lippert-Mataga, Bakhshiev, Kawski-Chamma-Viallet, and Reichardt) revealed a significantly higher excited-state d…

item.title: Stability of digital and analog quantum simulations under noise
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Jens Eisert, Jayant Rao, Tommaso Guaita
Quantum simulation is a central application of near-term quantum devices, pursued in both analog and digital architectures. A key challenge for both paradigms is the effect of imperfections and noise on predictive power. In this work, we present a rigorous and physically transparent comparison of the stability of digital and analog quantum simulators under a variety of perturbative noise models. We provide rigorous worst- and average-case error bounds for noisy quantum simulation of local observables. We find that the two paradigms show comparable scaling in the worst case, while exhibiting different forms of enhanced error cancellation on average. We further analyze Gaussian and Brownian noise processes, deriving concentration bounds that capture typical deviations beyond worst-case guar…

item.title: Process Tensor Approaches to Non-Markovian Quantum Dynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. X
👤 Jonathan Keeling, E. Miles Stoudenmire, Mari-Carmen Bañuls, and David R. Reichman
Author(s): Jonathan Keeling, E. Miles Stoudenmire, Mari-Carmen Bañuls, and David R. Reichman Researchers review the process tensor framework and demonstrate how efficient tensor-network representations enable the practical simulation of complex, non-Markovian quantum dynamics across diverse physical fields. [Phys. Rev. X 16, 020502] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Optimal work extraction from finite-time closed quantum dynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Shoki Sugimoto, Takahiro Sagawa, and Ryusuke Hamazaki
Author(s): Shoki Sugimoto, Takahiro Sagawa, and Ryusuke Hamazaki Extracting useful work from quantum systems is a fundamental problem in quantum thermodynamics. In scenarios where rapid protocols are desired—whether due to practical constraints or deliberate design choices—a fundamental trade-off between power and efficiency is yet to be established. Here, we inv… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023321] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Investigating quantum and molecular plumbing in nanofluidics research
item.description:
📚 Physics News
Our body contains an intricate system of tiny vessels through which blood, water and other molecules flow. When the size of the pipes shrinks to the nanoscale, where only a few molecules can fit side by side, the classical laws of physics governing the behavior of water are influenced by the atomic structure of the walls. "It's not that classical hydrodynamics breaks down, but rather that it gets mixed with the condensed matter physics of the solid walls," says Nikita Kavokine, tenure-track assistant professor and leader of the EPFL Quantum Plumbing Lab.

item.title: Quantum mechanics theory may work without imaginary numbers, new analysis suggests
item.description:
📚 Physics News
Physicists from Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf (HHU) have examined a fundamental property of quantum mechanics in collaboration with the German Aerospace Center (DLR). In an article published in the journal Physical Review Letters, they show that this theory does not necessarily need to be formulated with imaginary numbers—real numbers can, in fact, also be used.

item.title: Electrospinning Preparation of TiO2 Fiber Membranes and the Enhancement of Photocatalytic Uranium Reduction Efficiency by One-Dimensional Morphology
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Yongshuang You, Shuangyang Zhou, Xianqing Chen, Lisha Yang, Yan Liu, Xinke Dan 等10位作者
Nuclear energy as a key low-carbon, sustainable, and clean energy source, it's proportion in the global energy structure continues to increase. However, the rapid development of nuclear energy inevitably leads to the generation of a large amount of uranium-containing wastewater, posing serious risks to environment and health. Uranium in aqueous systems predominantly exists in the form of mobile and toxic U(VI) (UO22⁺), while U(IV) has very low solubility in water and is easier to remove. Photocatalytic reduction of highly soluble U(VI) to sparingly soluble U(IV) offers a promising route for uranium recovery. However, conventional photocatalysts with typically powder material that suffer from low activity, aggregation, and poor recyclability. Therefore, we developed a TiO2 fiber membranes…

item.title: Theory of high-harmonic generation with quantum light
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Haoyu Liu, Hanxu Zhang, Xu Wang, and Jianmin Yuan
Author(s): Haoyu Liu, Hanxu Zhang, Xu Wang, and Jianmin Yuan High-harmonic generation (HHG) driven by intense quantum light has recently emerged as a new frontier in strong-field physics. Yet, the mechanism underlying HHG with nonclassical fields such as bright squeezed vacuum (BSV) light—characterized by a vanishing mean electric field—remains elusive. In th… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023326] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: Edge state effects in quantum work statistics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Moallison F. Cavalcante
Author(s): Moallison F. Cavalcante Motivated by the objective of quantifying the energetic cost of accessing boundary phases through local control, we investigate here a simple, analytically tractable quantum impurity model. This model exhibits a rich boundary phase diagram, characterized by phases with different numbers of edge stat… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214321] Published Mon Jun 22, 2026

item.title: 用于隐私保护低功耗可穿戴健康监测的联邦时序 Kolmogorov-Arnold 网络
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Muhammad Imran Khalid, Sohaib Saleem, Ghulam Ali, Tamim Alkhalifah
该研究提出 Priv-Fed-X，一种面向电池供电可穿戴设备的隐私保护与能耗感知边缘智能框架。该框架结合轻量级时序 Kolmogorov-Arnold 网络、基于信噪比自适应门控的存储转发通信、差分隐私联邦学习以及事件触发的可解释性模块，以减少连续推理和通信的能耗。实验表明，该方案在保持有竞争力预测性能的同时提升了能效，适用于始终在线的隐私敏感健康监测场景。
